title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
summary: Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) standardizes how agents connect to data sources including BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB without requiring custom connectors. The framework integrates with Model Context Protocol (MCP) and includes AgentOps for observability and cost tracking.
writeup: The session demonstrates building AI agents with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) and connecting them to data sources through the Model Context Protocol (MCP), which standardizes agent-to-data connections. It walks through agents that access BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB in unified workflows, showing how MCP serves as a common interface across these systems. It also covers AgentOps for monitoring agent performance and cost with minimal code overhead, addressing the operational side of running agents in production.
